title: salvia rubescens subsp. truxillensis
intro: Salvia rubescens subsp. truxillensis, also known as Andean sage, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family native to the Andes mountains in South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salvia rubescens subsp. truxillensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or extracting the plant's active compounds using a solvent like ethanol.
